title: Getting Started with shigella
text: shigella package provides tools analyzing longitudinal antibody kinetics data Shigella infection studies. vignette introduces main functionality using mock data. Note: package currently uses mock data testing examples. Real Shigella datasets added future release.

title: Getting Started with shigella
text: process_shigella_data() function reshapes raw longitudinal data format compatible serodynamics package:
code: # Example with real data (not run) dat_long <- process_shigella_data(   data = raw_data,   study_filter = "SOSAR",   antigen = n_ipab_MFI )  # Convert to case_data format dL <- serodynamics::as_case_data(   dat_long,   id_var = "index_id",   biomarker_var = "antigen_iso",   time_in_days = "timeindays",   value_var = "result" )

title: Getting Started with shigella
text: package provides functions evaluate compare longitudinal antibody models:

title: Getting Started with shigella
code: # Compute residuals at individual level metrics_id <- compute_residual_metrics(   model = posterior_draws,   dataset = case_data,   ids = unique(case_data$id),   antigen_iso = "IgG",   scale = "original",   summary_level = "id_antigen" )  # Overall summary metrics metrics_overall <- compute_residual_metrics(   model = posterior_draws,   dataset = case_data,   ids = unique(case_data$id),   antigen_iso = "IgG",   scale = "original",   summary_level = "overall" )

title: Getting Started with shigella
text: Compare model fit metrics different modeling approaches:
code: comparison <- model_comparison_table(   metrics_overall = metrics_model1,   metrics_pointwise = metrics_model2,   model_overall_label = "Population Model",   model_pointwise_label = "Individual Model" )

title: Getting Started with shigella
text: fig2_overall_newperson() function creates trajectory plots population-level (“newperson”) predictions:
code: fig <- fig2_overall_newperson(   overall_models = list(     IpaB = model_ipab,     Sf2a = model_sf2a   ),   osps = c("IpaB", "Sf2a"),   isotypes = c("IgG", "IgA"),   t_grid = seq(0, 210, by = 5),   log_y = TRUE )

title: Getting Started with shigella
text: package includes two mock datasets testing: mock_posterior_draws: Posterior parameter draws long format mock_case_data: Longitudinal antibody measurements datasets structure real data contain synthetic values.
code: # Load mock data data("mock_posterior_draws") data("mock_case_data")  # View structure head(mock_posterior_draws) head(mock_case_data)

title: Getting Started with shigella
text: detailed examples analysis workflows, see: Package documentation: ?shigella Function help: ?compute_residual_metrics, ?process_shigella_data GitHub repository: https://github.com/UCD-SERG/shigella

what: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: repo Shigella analyses goal shigella …

what: Residual-based posterior predictive metrics for longitudinal antibody curves — compute_residual_metrics
title: Residual-based posterior predictive metrics for longitudinal antibody curves — compute_residual_metrics
text: Computes residuals observed antibody measurements posterior median predictions evaluated observed time points. Returns pointwise residuals aggregated error metrics (MAE, RMSE, SSE) multiple summary levels.

title: Residual-based posterior predictive metrics for longitudinal antibody curves — compute_residual_metrics
code: compute_residual_metrics(   model,   dataset,   ids,   antigen_iso,   scale = c("original", "log"),   summary_level = c("id_antigen", "pointwise", "antigen", "overall") )

title: Residual-based posterior predictive metrics for longitudinal antibody curves — compute_residual_metrics
text: model data frame posterior draws long format columns: Subject, Iso_type, Chain, Iteration, Parameter, value. dataset serodynamics case dataset produced serodynamics::as_case_data() (must contain id antigen_iso columns, time/value attributes). ids Character vector subject IDs include (matched dataset$id model$Subject). antigen_iso Character scalar specifying antigen/isotype analyze (matched dataset$antigen_iso model$Iso_type). scale Scale compute residuals. One "original" "log". "log", residuals computed natural log scale observations/predictions \(\le 0\) removed. summary_level Level summarize metrics. One : "pointwise" Return pointwise residuals observed time point. "id_antigen" Summarize id antigen_iso. "antigen" Summarize antigen_iso . "overall" Single summary across included observations.

title: Residual-based posterior predictive metrics for longitudinal antibody curves — compute_residual_metrics
text: tibble. summary_level = "pointwise", returns per-observation residuals. Otherwise returns MAE, RMSE, SSE, n_obs requested summary level.

title: Residual-based posterior predictive metrics for longitudinal antibody curves — compute_residual_metrics
text: posterior predictive summary uses posterior median observed time point, 95\ predictions. Predictions generated predict_posterior_at_times.

title: Residual-based posterior predictive metrics for longitudinal antibody curves — compute_residual_metrics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Per-ID error metrics (original scale) m_id <- compute_residual_metrics(   model = overall_sf2a,   dataset = dL_clean_sf2a,   ids = unique(dL_clean_sf2a$id),   antigen_iso = "IgG",   scale = "original",   summary_level = "id_antigen" )  # Pointwise residuals on log scale r_pw <- compute_residual_metrics(   model = overall_sf2a,   dataset = dL_clean_sf2a,   ids = "SOSAR-22008",   antigen_iso = "IgA",   scale = "log",   summary_level = "pointwise" ) } # }

title: Summarize population-level (
text: Computes median credible interval bands antibody trajectory hypothetical new individual drawn population distribution ("newperson").

title: Summarize population-level (
code: fig2_overall_newperson(   overall_models,   osps = c("IpaB", "Sf2a", "Sf3a", "Sf6", "Sonnei"),   ids = "newperson",   isotypes = c("IgG", "IgA"),   t_grid = seq(0, 210, by = 5),   cred = 0.95,   log_y = TRUE,   xlim = c(0, 210),   ylab = "Normalized MFI",   line_color = "#1f77b4",   ribbon_alpha = 0.2,   facet_scales = "fixed",   return_data = FALSE )

title: Summarize population-level (
text: overall_models Named list posterior draws long format (one per antigen), columns least: Subject, Iso_type, Chain, Iteration, Parameter, value. osps Character vector antigen names (must match names overall_models). ids Character vector Subject IDs include (default: "newperson"). isotypes Character vector isotypes (default: c("IgG", "IgA")). t_grid Numeric vector time points (days) evaluate. cred Credible level (default 0.95). log_y Logical; TRUE, applies log10 scale y returning plot. xlim Optional numeric length-2 vector x-axis limits returning plot. ylab Y-axis label plot. line_color Line color plot. ribbon_alpha Alpha credible ribbon plot. facet_scales Passed ggplot facet scales. return_data TRUE, returns list plot data.

title: Summarize population-level (
text: default, ggplot. return_data = TRUE, returns list(plot = p, data = df).

title: Summarize population-level (
text: Requires model draw can pivoted wide parameters including: y0, y1, t1, alpha, shape.

what: Format median and credible interval as a single string — fmt_mci
title: Format median and credible interval as a single string — fmt_mci
text: Format median credible interval single string

title: Format median and credible interval as a single string — fmt_mci
code: fmt_mci(med, lo, hi, digits = 2, sci = FALSE)

title: Format median and credible interval as a single string — fmt_mci
text: med Median value. lo Lower bound. hi Upper bound. digits Number digits. sci Logical; TRUE uses scientific notation.

title: Format median and credible interval as a single string — fmt_mci

what: Compare serotype-specific vs overall models using residual metrics — make_model_comparison_table
title: Compare serotype-specific vs overall models using residual metrics — make_model_comparison_table
text: Computes per-ID residual metrics two models intersection IDs present datasets, reports absolute percent differences.

title: Compare serotype-specific vs overall models using residual metrics — make_model_comparison_table
code: make_model_comparison_table(   model_serospecific,   data_serospecific,   model_overall,   data_overall,   antigen_iso,   scale = c("original", "log"),   tie_tol = 1e-08 )

title: Compare serotype-specific vs overall models using residual metrics — make_model_comparison_table
text: model_serospecific Posterior draws (long format) serotype-specific model. data_serospecific Case dataset used serotype-specific model. model_overall Posterior draws (long format) overall model. data_overall Case dataset used overall model. antigen_iso Character scalar antigen/isotype label. scale "original" "log". tie_tol Numeric tolerance declare tie.

title: Compare serotype-specific vs overall models using residual metrics — make_model_comparison_table
text: tibble per-ID MAE/RMSE model deltas/winner labels.

what: Mock case data for testing — mock_case_data
title: Mock case data for testing — mock_case_data
text: mock longitudinal antibody dataset compatible serodynamics::as_case_data(), testing functions like compute_residual_metrics.

title: Mock case data for testing — mock_case_data
code: mock_case_data

title: Mock case data for testing — mock_case_data
text: data frame class c("case_data", "data.frame") columns: id Character. Subject ID antigen_iso Character. Isotype (e.g., "IgG", "IgA") timepoint Numeric. Time days since infection value Numeric. Antibody measurement

title: Mock case data for testing — mock_case_data
text: synthetic data generated testing examples. Real Shigella case data added separately. dataset attributes: attr(mock_case_data, "timeindays") = "timepoint" attr(mock_case_data, "value_var") = "value"

title: Mock case data for testing — mock_case_data
code: head(mock_case_data) #>            id antigen_iso timepoint    value #> 1 SOSAR-22008         IgG         0 241.3326 #> 2 SOSAR-22015         IgG         0 152.5996 #> 3 SOSAR-22020         IgG         0 272.4704 #> 4 SOSAR-22025         IgG         0 327.1269 #> 5 SOSAR-22008         IgA         0 255.5663 #> 6 SOSAR-22015         IgA         0 277.2024 table(mock_case_data$id) #>  #> SOSAR-22008 SOSAR-22015 SOSAR-22020 SOSAR-22025  #>          12          12          12          12

what: Mock posterior draws for testing — mock_posterior_draws
title: Mock posterior draws for testing — mock_posterior_draws
text: mock dataset posterior parameter draws long format, mimicking structure expected functions like compute_residual_metrics predict_posterior_at_times.

title: Mock posterior draws for testing — mock_posterior_draws
code: mock_posterior_draws

title: Mock posterior draws for testing — mock_posterior_draws
text: data frame columns: Subject Character. Subject ID (e.g., "newperson", "SOSAR-22008") Iso_type Character. Isotype (e.g., "IgG", "IgA") Chain Integer. MCMC chain number Iteration Integer. MCMC iteration number Parameter Character. Parameter name (y0, y1, t1, alpha, shape) value Numeric. Parameter value

title: Mock posterior draws for testing — mock_posterior_draws
text: synthetic data generated testing examples. Real Shigella posterior draws added package separately. Parameters represent: y0: Baseline antibody level y1: Peak antibody level t1: Time peak (days) alpha: Decay rate parameter shape: Decay shape parameter (rho)

title: Mock posterior draws for testing — mock_posterior_draws
code: head(mock_posterior_draws) #>       Subject Iso_type Chain Iteration Parameter    value #> 1   newperson      IgG     1         1        y0 434.7770 #> 2 SOSAR-22008      IgG     1         1        y0 228.3470 #> 3 SOSAR-22015      IgG     1         1        y0 372.1453 #> 4   newperson      IgA     1         1        y0 379.2692 #> 5 SOSAR-22008      IgA     1         1        y0 282.8037 #> 6 SOSAR-22015      IgA     1         1        y0 380.5681 table(mock_posterior_draws$Parameter) #>  #> alpha shape    t1    y0    y1  #>  1800  1800  1800  1800  1800

what: Compare summary residual metrics between two model outputs — model_comparison_table
title: Compare summary residual metrics between two model outputs — model_comparison_table
text: Builds compact comparison table pre-computed summary metrics overall model pointwise model.

title: Compare summary residual metrics between two model outputs — model_comparison_table
code: model_comparison_table(   metrics_overall,   metrics_pointwise,   model_overall_label = "Overall Model",   model_pointwise_label = "Pointwise Model" )

title: Compare summary residual metrics between two model outputs — model_comparison_table
text: metrics_overall Data frame one row containing least MAE, RMSE, SSE, n_obs overall model. metrics_pointwise Data frame one row containing least MAE, RMSE, SSE, n_obs pointwise model. model_overall_label Label used overall model row. model_pointwise_label Label used pointwise model row.

title: Compare summary residual metrics between two model outputs — model_comparison_table
text: tibble one row per model comparison columns: delta_MAE, delta_RMSE, pct_improve_MAE, pct_improve_RMSE.

what: Posterior predictions at specified times for given subjects and antigen/isotype — predict_posterior_at_times
title: Posterior predictions at specified times for given subjects and antigen/isotype — predict_posterior_at_times
text: Generates draw-level posterior predictions antibody trajectory user-specified time points, one subjects selected antigen/isotype. low-level helper used residual-based posterior predictive diagnostics.

title: Posterior predictions at specified times for given subjects and antigen/isotype — predict_posterior_at_times
code: predict_posterior_at_times(model, ids, antigen_iso, times)

title: Posterior predictions at specified times for given subjects and antigen/isotype — predict_posterior_at_times
text: model data frame posterior draws long format columns: Subject, Iso_type, Chain, Iteration, Parameter, value. ids Character vector subject IDs include (matched Subject). antigen_iso Character scalar specifying antigen/isotype include (matched Iso_type). times Numeric vector time points (days) evaluate predictions.

title: Posterior predictions at specified times for given subjects and antigen/isotype — predict_posterior_at_times
text: tibble one row per (posterior draw \(\times\) time \(\times\) subject), including evaluated prediction res. Output includes least: id Subject ID (character). t Time (days) prediction evaluated. Chain MCMC chain index (present model). Iteration MCMC iteration index (present model). sample_id Row index draw (added missing). y0, y1, t1, alpha, shape Model parameters (wide). res Predicted antibody level time t.

title: Posterior predictions at specified times for given subjects and antigen/isotype — predict_posterior_at_times
text: function pivots posterior draws wide format (parameters columns), expands times, evaluates antibody curve via internal implementation antibody kinetics model using parameters y0, y1, t1, alpha, shape.

title: Posterior predictions at specified times for given subjects and antigen/isotype — predict_posterior_at_times
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ preds <- predict_posterior_at_times(   model = overall_sf2a,   ids = "newperson",   antigen_iso = "IgG",   times = c(0, 30, 90, 180) ) } # }

title: Extract
text: Extract "newperson" parameter draws summarize Table 2

title: Extract
code: prep_newperson_params(draws_long, antigen_label)

title: Extract
text: draws_long Posterior draws long format columns: Subject, Iso_type, Chain, Iteration, Parameter, value. antigen_label Character scalar antigen name attach.

title: Extract
text: tibble newperson draws wide format columns: Iteration, Chain, antigen, Iso_type, y0, y1, t1, alpha, rho.

what: Reshape Shigella longitudinal data for serodynamics workflows — process_shigella_data
title: Reshape Shigella longitudinal data for serodynamics workflows — process_shigella_data
text: Filters dataset given study antigen column, standardizes column names, returns visit-ordered long dataset suitable conversion serodynamics::as_case_data().

title: Reshape Shigella longitudinal data for serodynamics workflows — process_shigella_data
code: process_shigella_data(data, study_filter, antigen)

title: Reshape Shigella longitudinal data for serodynamics workflows — process_shigella_data
text: data data frame containing longitudinal measurements. study_filter Character scalar. Value study_name keep (e.g. "SOSAR"). antigen Unquoted column name antigen measurement (e.g. n_ipab_MFI).

title: Reshape Shigella longitudinal data for serodynamics workflows — process_shigella_data
text: tibble standardized columns: index_id Participant ID (copied sid). antigen_iso Isotype label (copied isotype_name). visit Visit label (copied timepoint). timeindays Time since infection (copied Actual day). result Antibody measurement (antigen).

title: Reshape Shigella longitudinal data for serodynamics workflows — process_shigella_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dat_long <- process_shigella_data(df, "SOSAR", n_ipab_MFI) dL <- serodynamics::as_case_data(dat_long,   id_var = "index_id", biomarker_var = "antigen_iso",   time_in_days = "timeindays", value_var = "result" ) } # }
